subject: physics
summary_en: Magnetic field $\vec{B}$ exerts force on moving charges: $\vec{F} = q\vec{v} \times \vec{B}$. On current-carrying wires: $\vec{F} = I\vec{L} \times \vec{B}$. Magnetic forces do NO work — they only change direction of motion.
summary_he: שדה מגנטי $\vec{B}$ מפעיל כוח על מטענים נעים: $\vec{F} = q\vec{v} \times \vec{B}$. על תיל זרם: $\vec{F} = I\vec{L} \times \vec{B}$. כוחות מגנטיים **לא** עושים עבודה.

sections.body_en_md:
Magnets stick to fridges. Compass needles point north. Speakers vibrate. All of this is **magnetism**.

Unlike gravity or the electric field, magnetism affects only MOVING charges (and the current-carrying wires they form).

### Properties of $\vec{B}$ field

- Symbol: $\vec{B}$. Unit: **tesla** (T). $1 \, \text{T} = 1 \, \text{kg}/(\text{A} \cdot \text{s}^2)$.
- Earth's field: $\sim 50 \, \mu\text{T}$ at surface.
- Strong magnet (MRI): $\sim 1.5$ T.
- Neutron star: $10^{10}$ T.

### Sources

- Moving charges (currents in wires).
- Spinning electrons in atoms (basis of ferromagnetism in iron, nickel).

### Field lines

- Closed loops (unlike electric field lines which start/end on charges).
- From N-pole to S-pole OUTSIDE the magnet; S to N INSIDE.
- **No magnetic monopoles** — every magnet has both N and S.
sections.body_he_md:
מגנטים נדבקים למקרר. מצפן מצביע לצפון. רמקולים רוטטים. הכל **מגנטיות**.

שונה מכבידה/חשמלי: משפיע **רק על מטענים נעים** (וזרמים).

### תכונות $\vec{B}$

- יחידה: **טסלה** (T).
- שדה כדור הארץ: $\sim 50\,\mu$T.
- MRI: $\sim 1.5$ T.
- כוכב נויטרונים: $10^{10}$ T.

### מקורות

- מטענים נעים (זרמים).
- ספין אלקטרונים (פרומגנטיות).

### קווי שדה

- לולאות סגורות.
- מ-N ל-S בחוץ.
- **אין מונופולים** — כל מגנט יש לו N ו-S.

hs_physics.body_en_md:
Magnetic fields are vector fields that surround magnetic materials and exert forces on moving charges and currents. The direction of the magnetic field is defined by the right-hand rule, where the thumb points in the direction of the current and the fingers curl in the direction of the magnetic field. The force on a moving charge is given by $F = qv \times B$, where $F$ is the force, $q$ is the charge, $v$ is the velocity of the charge, and $B$ is the magnetic field strength.

The force on a current-carrying wire is given by $F = BIL$, where $F$ is the force, $B$ is the magnetic field strength, $I$ is the current, and $L$ is the length of the wire. The magnetic field of a straight wire is given by $B = \frac{\mu_0 I}{2 \pi r}$, where $B$ is the magnetic field strength, $\mu_0$ is the magnetic constant ($4\pi \times 10^{-7} \text{ Tm/A}$), $I$ is the current, and $r$ is the distance from the wire.

Worked example:
A wire of length 2 m carries a current of 5 A and is placed in a magnetic field of strength 0.1 T. What is the force on the wire?
$$F = BIL = (0.1 \text{ T})(5 \text{ A})(2 \text{ m}) = 1 \text{ N}$$
hs_physics.body_he_md: שדות מגנטיים הם שדות וקטוריים הסובבים חומרים מגנטיים ומפעילים כוחות על טענות נעות ועל זרמים. הכיוון של שדה המגנטי הוא נקבע על ידי כלל הימין, שבו האצבע המורה נקבעת בכיוון של

sections.title_en: Lorentz force, wire force, motion in fields
sections.title_he: כוח לורנץ, כוח על תיל, תנועה בשדות
sections.body_en_md:
### Lorentz force (single charge)

$$\boxed{\vec{F} = q \vec{v} \times \vec{B}.}$$

Magnitude: $F = |q| v B \sin\theta$ (where $\theta$ = angle between $\vec{v}$ and $\vec{B}$).

Direction: by RIGHT-HAND RULE.
- Point fingers in direction of $\vec{v}$.
- Curl them toward $\vec{B}$.
- Thumb gives direction of $\vec{F}$ for POSITIVE charge.
- For negative: opposite.

**Key:** If $\vec{v} \parallel \vec{B}$ (parallel), no force. Force is ALWAYS perpendicular to $\vec{v}$.

### No work

$\vec{F} \perp \vec{v} \Rightarrow \vec{F} \cdot \vec{v} = 0$. Magnetic force does NO WORK; speed (and kinetic energy) is unchanged. Only direction changes.

### Circular motion of charge in uniform $B$

If $\vec{v} \perp \vec{B}$: charge moves in a CIRCLE. Magnetic force = centripetal:

$$qvB = \frac{mv^2}{r} \Rightarrow \boxed{r = \frac{mv}{|q|B}.}$$

Frequency of circular motion (cyclotron frequency):

$$f_c = \frac{|q|B}{2\pi m}.$$

Used in mass spectrometers and particle accelerators.

### Force on current-carrying wire

Wire of length $L$ carrying current $I$ in field $\vec{B}$:

$$\boxed{\vec{F} = I \vec{L} \times \vec{B}, \quad F = I L B \sin\theta.}$$

Direction: right-hand rule (using $\vec{L}$ in direction of $I$).

This is how electric motors work.

### Field of a long straight wire

At distance $d$ from an infinite straight wire carrying current $I$:

$$B = \frac{\mu_0 I}{2 \pi d},$$

where $\mu_0 = 4\pi \times 10^{-7} \, \text{T} \cdot \text{m/A}$ (permeability of free space).

Field CIRCLES around the wire (right-hand rule: thumb in $I$, fingers curl in $\vec{B}$).

### Solenoid field

Inside a long solenoid (many turns): $B = \mu_0 n I$, where $n$ = turns per unit length. Field is roughly uniform inside.
sections.body_he_md:
### כוח לורנץ

$$\boxed{\vec{F} = q\vec{v} \times \vec{B}.}$$

גודל: $F = |q|vB\sin\theta$. כיוון: יד ימין.

**מפתח:** $\vec{v} \parallel \vec{B}$ → אין כוח. $\vec{F}$ תמיד ניצב ל-$\vec{v}$.

### אין עבודה

$\vec{F} \perp \vec{v} \Rightarrow$ אין עבודה. אנרגיה קינטית נשמרת.

### תנועה מעגלית

אם $\vec{v} \perp \vec{B}$:

$$r = mv/(|q|B).$$

תדירות ציקלוטרון: $f_c = |q|B/(2\pi m)$.

### כוח על תיל זרם

$$\boxed{F = ILB\sin\theta.}$$

בסיס למנועים חשמליים.

### שדה תיל ארוך

$$B = \mu_0 I / (2\pi d).$$

$\mu_0 = 4\pi \times 10^{-7}$ T·m/A. שדה סובב סביב התיל.

### שדה סולנואיד

$B = \mu_0 n I$ בפנים.

hs_physics.body_en_md:
Magnetic Force:
The magnetic force on a moving charge is given by the Lorentz force equation: $$\vec{F} = q(\vec{v} \times \vec{B})$$, where $q$ is the charge, $\vec{v}$ is the velocity of the charge, and $\vec{B}$ is the magnetic field. The direction of the force is perpendicular to both the velocity and the magnetic field, and can be determined using the right-hand rule.

Force on Current:
The force on a current-carrying wire is given by: $$\vec{F} = \vec{B} \times \vec{I}L$$, where $I$ is the current, $L$ is the length of the wire, and $\vec{B}$ is the magnetic field. Alternatively, it can be expressed as: $$\vec{F} = BIL \sin(\theta)$$, where $\theta$ is the angle between the wire and the magnetic field.

Magnetic Field of a Straight Wire:
The magnetic field of a straight wire is given by: $$B = \frac{\mu_0 I}{2 \pi r}$$, where $\mu_0$ is the magnetic constant ($4 \pi \times 10^{-7} \, \text{Tm/A}$), $I$ is the current, and $r$ is the distance from the wire.

Worked Example:
A charge of $2 \times 10^{-6} \, \text{C}$ is moving at a velocity of $5 \, \text{m/s}$ in a magnetic field of $0.5 \, \text{T}$. Determine the magnitude of the magnetic force on the charge.

Given: $q = 2 \times 10^{-6} \, \text{C}$, $v = 5 \, \text{m/s}$, $B = 0.5 \, \text{T}$

Find: $F$

Formula: $$F = qvB$$

Answer: $F = (2 \times 10^{-6} \, \text{C})(5 \, \text{m/s})(0.5 \, \text{T}) = 5 \times 10^{-6} \, \text{N}$

sections.body_en_md:
An electron moves at $v = 2 \times 10^6$ m/s perpendicular to a 0.01 T magnetic field. Find (a) the magnetic force magnitude, (b) the radius of its circular path, (c) cyclotron frequency.

**(a) Force:** $F = |q|vB = (1.6 \times 10^{-19})(2 \times 10^6)(0.01) = 3.2 \times 10^{-15}$ N.

**(b) Radius:** $r = mv/(|q|B) = (9.11 \times 10^{-31})(2 \times 10^6)/[(1.6 \times 10^{-19})(0.01)]$.

$$r = (1.822 \times 10^{-24})/(1.6 \times 10^{-21}) \approx 1.14 \times 10^{-3} \, \text{m} = 1.14 \, \text{mm}.$$

**(c) Frequency:** $f_c = |q|B/(2\pi m) = (1.6 \times 10^{-19})(0.01)/[2\pi (9.11 \times 10^{-31})]$.

$$f_c = (1.6 \times 10^{-21})/(5.72 \times 10^{-30}) \approx 2.80 \times 10^8 \, \text{Hz} = 280 \, \text{MHz}.$$

**Answer:** $F \approx 3.2 \times 10^{-15}$ N, $r \approx 1.14$ mm, $f_c \approx 280$ MHz.

Notice: an electron in 0.01 T spirals at ~280 MHz — the basis of microwave electronics.
sections.body_he_md:
אלקטרון ב-$v = 2\times10^6$ מ"ש ניצב לשדה $B = 0.01$ T. (א) כוח, (ב) רדיוס, (ג) תדירות?

**(א)** $F = (1.6\times10^{-19})(2\times10^6)(0.01) = 3.2\times10^{-15}$ N.
**(ב)** $r = mv/(|q|B) \approx 1.14$ מ"מ.
**(ג)** $f_c \approx 280$ MHz.

hs_physics.body_en_md:
Magnetic force on a moving charge:
The magnetic force on a moving charge is given by the equation $F = qv \times B$, where $F$ is the force, $q$ is the charge, $v$ is the velocity of the charge, and $B$ is the magnetic field strength. The direction of the force is perpendicular to both the velocity and the magnetic field, and can be determined using the right-hand rule.

The force on a current-carrying wire is given by $F = BIL$, where $I$ is the current and $L$ is the length of the wire.

The magnetic field of a straight wire is given by $B = \frac{\mu_0 I}{2 \pi r}$, where $\mu_0$ is the magnetic constant ($4 \pi \times 10^{-7} \, \text{Tm/A}$), $I$ is the current, and $r$ is the distance from the wire.

Worked example:
An electron with a charge of $-1.6 \times 10^{-19} \, \text{C}$ is moving at a velocity of $2 \times 10^6 \, \text{m/s}$ in a magnetic field of $0.5 \, \text{T}$. Find the magnetic force on the electron.

Given: $q = -1.6 \times 10^{-19} \, \text{C}$, $v = 2 \times 10^6 \, \text{m/s}$, $B = 0.5 \, \text{T}$

Find: $F$

Formula: $F = qv \times B$

Answer: $F = (-1.6 \times 10^{-19} \, \text{C})(2 \times 10^6 \, \text{m/s}) \times (0.5 \, \text{T}) = -1.6 \times 10^{-13} \, \text{N}$
hs_physics.body_he_md:
כוח מגנטי על טעון נע:
הכוח המגנטי על טעון נע נתון על ידי המשוואה $F = qv \times B

sections.body_en_md:
1. **'Magnetic force can do work.'** NO. $\vec{F} \perp \vec{v}$, so $\vec{F} \cdot \vec{v} = 0$.
2. **Direction of force.** Right-hand rule for $\vec{v} \times \vec{B}$. For negative charge: flip.
3. **Stationary charge in field.** No force ($v = 0$).
4. **$\vec{v} \parallel \vec{B}$.** No force ($\sin 0 = 0$).
5. **Field lines: start/end?** They don't (closed loops; no monopoles).
6. **Confusing $\vec{B}$ with $\vec{E}$.** They have different units (T vs. N/C) and different force laws.
7. **Forgetting $\sin\theta$.** $F = qvB\sin\theta$. Only perpendicular component contributes.
sections.body_he_md:
1. **'כוח מגנטי עושה עבודה'** — לא.
2. **כיוון כוח** — יד ימין; שלילי הפוך.
3. **מטען נח** — אין כוח.
4. **$\vec{v}\parallel\vec{B}$** — אין כוח.
5. **קווי שדה לא מתחילים/מסתיימים.**
6. **$\vec{B}$ מול $\vec{E}$.**
7. **לא לשכוח $\sin\theta$.**

hs_physics.body_en_md:
Magnetic force on a moving charge is given by the equation $F = qvB \sin \theta$, where $F$ is the force in $\text{N}$, $q$ is the charge in $\text{C}$, $v$ is the velocity in $\text{m/s}$, $B$ is the magnetic field strength in $\text{T}$, and $\theta$ is the angle between the velocity and the magnetic field. The direction of the force is given by the right-hand rule.

The force on a current-carrying wire is given by $F = BIL \sin \theta$, where $F$ is the force in $\text{N}$, $B$ is the magnetic field strength in $\text{T}$, $I$ is the current in $\text{A}$, $L$ is the length of the wire in $\text{m}$, and $\theta$ is the angle between the wire and the magnetic field.

The magnetic field of a straight wire is given by $B = \frac{\mu_0 I}{2 \pi r}$, where $B$ is the magnetic field strength in $\text{T}$, $\mu_0$ is the magnetic constant in $\text{N/A^2}$, $I$ is the current in $\text{A}$, and $r$ is the distance from the wire in $\text{m}$.
hs_physics.body_he_md:
כוח מגנטי על טעון נע נתון על ידי המשוואה $F = qvB \sin \theta$, שם $F$ הוא הכוח ב-$\text{N}$, $q$ הוא הטעון ב-$\text{C}$, $v$ הוא המהירות ב-$\text{m/s}$, $B$ הוא עוצמת השדה המגנטי ב-$\text{T}$, ו-$\theta$ הוא הזווית בין המהירות לשדה המגנטי. 

הכוח על חוט זר

sections.body_en_md:
- $\vec{F} = q\vec{v} \times \vec{B}$, $F = |q|vB\sin\theta$.
- Direction by right-hand rule.
- Magnetic force does NO work; only deflects.
- Circular motion: $r = mv/(|q|B)$.
- Wire force: $\vec{F} = I\vec{L}\times\vec{B}$.
- Wire field: $B = \mu_0 I/(2\pi d)$.
- Solenoid: $B = \mu_0 n I$.
- No magnetic monopoles.
sections.body_he_md:
- $\vec{F} = q\vec{v}\times\vec{B}$.
- יד ימין.
- אין עבודה.
- $r = mv/(|q|B)$.
- תיל: $F = ILB\sin\theta$.
- שדה תיל: $\mu_0 I/(2\pi d)$.
- סולנואיד: $\mu_0 nI$.

hs_physics.body_en_md:
Magnetic force on a moving charge is given by the equation $F = qvB\sin\theta$, where $F$ is the force in Newtons (N), $q$ is the charge in Coulombs (C), $v$ is the velocity in meters per second (m/s), $B$ is the magnetic field strength in Tesla (T), and $\theta$ is the angle between the velocity and magnetic field. The direction of the force can be determined using the right-hand rule.

The force on a current-carrying wire is given by $F = BIL$, where $F$ is the force in Newtons (N), $B$ is the magnetic field strength in Tesla (T), $I$ is the current in Amperes (A), and $L$ is the length of the wire in meters (m).

The magnetic field of a straight wire can be calculated using the equation $B = \frac{\mu_0I}{2\pi r}$, where $B$ is the magnetic field strength in Tesla (T), $\mu_0$ is the magnetic constant (4π x 10^-7 Tm/A), $I$ is the current in Amperes (A), and $r$ is the distance from the wire in meters (m).
hs_physics.body_he_md:
כוח מגנטי על טעון נע ניתן לחישוב באמצעות המשוואה $F = qvB\sin\theta$, שם $F$ הוא הכוח בניוטון (N), $q$ הוא הטעון בקולומב (C), $v$ הוא המהירות במטרים לשנייה (m/s), $B$ הוא עוצמת השדה המגנטי בטסלה (T), ו-$\theta$ הוא הזווית בין המהירות לשדה המגנטי. 

הכוח על חוט הע

sections.body_en_md:
### How to approach Bagrut-style questions

In the Bagrut exam, you will see multi-part questions (a, b, c) where each part builds on the previous. You must:
- **Show all steps** — the examiner grades your process, not just the answer
- **Write complete sentences** for proofs and justifications
- **Check your answer** makes sense before moving on

---

**Practice Problem 1:**

A 12 V battery connects $R_1=4\,\Omega$ in series with parallel $R_2=6\,\Omega$ and $R_3=3\,\Omega$. Find current through each resistor.

**Complete Solution:**

$R_{23}=\frac{6\cdot3}{6+3}=2\,\Omega$. Total $R=6\,\Omega$. $I=2$ A through $R_1$.
$V_{23}=8$ V → $I_2=\frac{4}{3}$ A, $I_3=\frac{8}{3}$ A.

---

**Practice Problem 2:**

Two resistors $3\,\Omega$ and $6\,\Omega$ in parallel on 9 V. Find power in each.

**Complete Solution:**

$I_1=3$ A, $P_1=27$ W; $I_2=1.5$ A, $P_2=13.5$ W.
sections.body_he_md:
### איך לגשת לשאלות בסגנון בגרות

בבחינת הבגרות תראו שאלות רב-סעיפיות (א, ב, ג) שכל סעיף נשען על הקודם. חובה:
- **להראות את כל השלבים** — הבוחן מדרג את הדרך, לא רק את התשובה
- **לכתוב משפטים מלאים** בהוכחות ובנימוקים
- **לבדוק שהתשובה הגיונית** לפני שממשיכים

---

**שאלת תרגול 1:**

12V, $R_1=4\Omega$ בטור עם $R_2=6\Omega$ ו-$R_3=3\Omega$ במקביל. זרמים?

**פתרון:** $I_1=2$ A, $I_2=\frac{4}{3}$ A, $I_3=\frac{8}{3}$ A.

---

**שאלת תרגול 2:**

$3\Omega$ ו-$6\Omega$ במקביל על 9V — הספקים?

**פתרון:** 27W ו-13.5W.

questions.stem_en: A stationary positive charge sits in a uniform $\vec{B}$ field. The magnetic force on it is:
questions.stem_he: מטען חיובי **נח** בשדה $\vec{B}$ אחיד. הכוח המגנטי עליו:

questions.stem_en: Magnetic force on a moving charge:
A proton moves at $10^6$ m/s perpendicular to a 0.2 T field. Find the magnetic force magnitude.
questions.stem_he: פרוטון נע ב-$10^6$ מ"ש ניצב לשדה 0.2 T. גודל כוח?
questions.rubric_en:
Full credit (2/2): $F = qvB = (1.6\times10^{-19})(10^6)(0.2) = 3.2\times10^{-14}$ N.
Partial (1/2): correct formula.

questions.stem_en: A charged particle enters a uniform $\vec{B}$ field perpendicular to $\vec{v}$. Its trajectory is:
questions.stem_he: חלקיק טעון נכנס לשדה $\vec{B}$ אחיד ניצב ל-$\vec{v}$. המסלול:
questions.explanation_en: $\vec{F}$ always perpendicular to $\vec{v}$ → uniform circular motion.

questions.stem_en:
A ball is launched horizontally at 15 m/s from a height of 20 m ($g=10$ m/s²).

(a) Time until it hits the ground.
(b) Horizontal distance traveled.
(c) Speed at impact (magnitude and direction).
questions.stem_he:
כדור נזרק אופקית ב-15 מ"ש מגובה 20 מ' ($g=10$).

(א) זמן עד פגיעה.
(ב) מרחק אופקי.
(ג) מהירות בפגיעה.
questions.rubric_en: 3pts time; 3pts distance; 4pts velocity components and magnitude.
questions.rubric_he: 3 נק' זמן; 3 נק' מרחק; 4 נק' מהירות.
questions.explanation_en: (a) 2 s (b) 30 m (c) 25 m/s at arctan(4/3) below horizontal.

questions.stem_en:
A mass $m$ on an incline at angle $\theta$ with coefficient of friction $\mu$.

(a) Draw a free-body diagram.
(b) Find acceleration down the slope.
(c) Find the friction force.
questions.stem_he:
מסה $m$ על משופע $\theta$ עם $\mu$.

(א) FBD.
(ב) תאוצה.
(ג) חיכוך.
questions.rubric_he: 3 נק' FBD; 4 נק' F=ma; 3 נק' חיכוך.
questions.explanation_en: a = g(sinθ - μcosθ), f = μmg cosθ.
questions.explanation_he: a = g(sinθ - μcosθ), f = μmg cosθ.

key_insights: Magnetic field affects only MOVING charges.
Right-hand rule for direction (negative charge: flip).
Magnetic force does NO WORK; only changes direction.

common_misconceptions.wrong: Magnetic force can speed up a charge
common_misconceptions.correction: Magnetic force is always perpendicular to $\vec{v}$ → no work → no speed change. Only direction changes.

agent_hints.tutor_pacing_hint: Stress: $\vec{B}$ affects ONLY moving charges. Right-hand rule needs practice — demo with hands. Magnetic force does no work; this is non-intuitive but crucial.

diagnostic_signals.if_learner_thinks_b_does_work: $\vec{F} \perp \vec{v}$ always. Imagine pushing perpendicular to motion — never speeds it up.
diagnostic_signals.if_learner_uses_left_hand: Right-hand rule (for positive charge). For negative, flip.
